title: regreSSHion
subtitle: OpenSSH unauthenticated RCE
query: Triage CVE-2024-6387 (regreSSHion) in OpenSSH and tell me the remediation urgency.

data.query: Triage CVE-2024-6387 (regreSSHion) in OpenSSH and tell me the remediation urgency.

data.summary: CVE-2024-6387 ("regreSSHion") is a race condition in OpenSSH sshd enabling unauthenticated RCE as root on glibc Linux — a regression of CVE-2006-5051. EPSS is 99.5% (99.94th percentile), a public exploit exists on Exploit-DB, and CVSS v3 is 8.1 HIGH. Not yet in CISA KEV, but exploitation risk is among the highest ever recorded.
data.recommended_action: SSVC: Attend (treat as Act). Public exploit (Exploit-DB #52269) + EPSS 99.5% demands out-of-cycle patching. Upgrade OpenSSH to 9.8p1+; macOS users: patch to 12.7.6 / 13.6.8 / 14.6. Interim: set LoginGraceTime 0 in sshd_config and restrict SSH to trusted IPs via firewall. Apply OS-vendor patches (RHEL, Ubuntu, AlmaLinux) immediately.

cves.summary: Race condition in OpenSSH sshd SIGALRM handler allows unauthenticated remote RCE as root on glibc-based Linux. Regression of CVE-2006-5051, dubbed regreSSHion. Discovered by Qualys.

reasoning_chain: cve_lookup(CVE-2024-6387) -> CVSS 8.1 HIGH; CWE-362 (race condition), CWE-364 (signal handler race); unauthenticated RCE in sshd
patch_lookup(CVE-2024-6387) -> OpenSSH fixed in 9.8p1; macOS fixed in 12.7.6/13.6.8/14.6
attack_mapping([CWE-362, CWE-364]) -> no curated ATT&CK mappings returned
Decision: SSVC Attend/Act — public exploit + EPSS>=0.995; out-of-cycle patching required

ssvc.rationale: SSVC decision Act: CVE-2024-6387 a public exploit is available and EPSS predicts high near-term exploitation.

signal_coverage.detail: CWE-362/CWE-364 not in curated mapping table
